steps.title: Identify the consequence relationship
steps.content: The first sentence establishes that Le Guin wrote both science fiction and fantasy. The second sentence explains that readers of both genres are among her audience. The relationship between them is cause-and-effect: the second logically results from the first.

steps.title: Apply the relationship to the choices
steps.content:
Accordingly expresses a result or consequence: because she wrote both genres, fans of both naturally became her readers. Alternatively suggests a choice where there is none, Nevertheless creates a false contrast, and At that time is purely temporal and irrelevant.

This matches choice B — B is correct.
